title: Add after field to the tick data model and JSON schema
description:
Add a soft-ordering field to the tick data model, sibling to BlockedBy.

Context: internal/tick/tick.go defines the Tick struct; BlockedBy is []string with json tag "blocked_by,omitempty" (line ~68). schemas/tick.schema.json is the JSON schema for tick files stored in .tick/issues/.

Work:
- Add After []string with json tag "after,omitempty" to the Tick struct, directly after BlockedBy.
- Add the after property to schemas/tick.schema.json mirroring blocked_by (optional array of strings).
- Doc comment must state the semantics: after expresses preferred ordering only (work these targets first if feasible); it never gates readiness — that is blocked_by. Entries are tick IDs; consumers ignore missing or closed targets.

Test cases (extend internal/tick tests):
- Marshal a Tick with After ["abc"] -> JSON contains "after":["abc"].
- Marshal a Tick with empty/nil After -> JSON omits the after key.
- Unmarshal {"after":["abc","def"]} -> After == ["abc","def"].
- Validate() on a tick with After set -> no new errors (shape-only, like blocked_by).

Files likely touched: internal/tick/tick.go, internal/tick/tick_test.go, schemas/tick.schema.json.
Run: go test ./internal/tick/...
acceptance_criteria: after round-trips through JSON and is omitted when empty; schema lists the property; go test ./internal/tick/... passes
